label: WTC 1 (North Tower)
sub: American Airlines Flight 11 struck floors 93–99 at 8:46 AM, killing all 92 aboard and hundreds inside. The tower stood for 102 minutes before collapsing at 10:28 AM, killing hundreds more including 343 FDNY personnel.

label: WTC 2 (South Tower)
sub: United Airlines Flight 175 struck floors 77–85 at 9:03 AM, live on television worldwide. Uniquely, one stairwell (Stairwell A) remained passable, allowing some above the impact zone to escape. The tower collapsed first at 9:59 AM.

label: WTC 7
sub: A 47-story skyscraper housing the NYC OEM, CIA and DoD offices, and SEC records. Ignited by debris from the North Tower, WTC 7 burned without suppression and collapsed at 5:20 PM — the first skyscraper in history to collapse primarily due to fire according to NIST.

label: The Pentagon
sub: American Airlines Flight 77 struck the western facade at 9:37 AM, killing 125 inside the building and all 64 aboard the aircraft. The recently renovated section featured blast-resistant windows that limited casualties compared to older sections.

label: Flight 93 Crash Site
sub: United Airlines Flight 93 crashed in a reclaimed strip mine field near Shanksville, Pennsylvania at 10:03 AM. All 44 passengers and crew were killed. Passengers who voted to storm the cockpit are believed to have prevented a strike on the U.S. Capitol or White House.

label: Boston Logan Airport
sub: Departure point for American Airlines Flight 11 (North Tower) and United Airlines Flight 175 (South Tower). A total of 10 hijackers boarded flights here. The airport's security procedures and pre-flight screening failures were scrutinized in the 9/11 Commission Report.

label: Newark Liberty Airport
sub: Departure point for United Airlines Flight 93. Four hijackers, including Ziad Jarrah (the pilot), boarded here. Flight 93 departed 42 minutes late, which gave passengers time to learn about the other attacks via phone calls and organize a passenger revolt.

label: Washington Dulles International Airport
sub: Departure point for American Airlines Flight 77 (Pentagon). Five hijackers including Mohammed Atta associate Hani Hanjour (the pilot) boarded here. Security camera footage shows hijackers passing through security checkpoints, which became key evidence in the 9/11 investigation.

label: FBI New York Field Office
sub: The FBI's New York field office led the PENTTBOM investigation — the largest in FBI history — examining the 9/11 attacks. Critical pre-9/11 warnings from the NY field office about suspicious flight school activity by Zacarias Moussaoui were not acted upon, a finding highlighted by the 9/11 Commission.

label: CIA Headquarters, Langley, VA
sub: The CIA's failure to share intelligence on al-Qaeda operatives Nawaf al-Hazmi and Khalid al-Mihdhar — who were known to be in the U.S. before 9/11 — is a central finding of the 9/11 Commission. The CIA also ran the post-9/11 detention and interrogation program in secret overseas black sites.

label: Fresh Kills Landfill (WTC Evidence Site)
sub: Over 1.5 million tons of WTC debris were processed here on Staten Island. Workers using hand-sifting and mechanical sorting recovered hundreds of thousands of personal artifacts and approximately 300 human remains. The evidence processing operation ran from September 2001 to July 2002.

label: National 9/11 Memorial
sub: Two massive reflecting pools set within the footprints of the Twin Towers, opened September 11, 2011. The names of all 2,983 victims (including 1993 WTC bombing victims) are inscribed on bronze parapets surrounding the pools. Designed by architect Michael Arad with landscape architect Peter Walker.

label: National 9/11 Memorial Museum
sub: Built 70 feet underground beneath the memorial pools, the museum opened May 21, 2014. It houses over 10,000 artifacts including the Survivors' Stairs, a destroyed fire truck, and FDNY communications records. The museum receives over 3 million visitors annually and preserves the oral histories of survivors.

label: Pentagon Memorial
sub: An outdoor memorial opened September 11, 2008, honoring the 184 victims killed at the Pentagon. The memorial features 184 illuminated benches — one for each victim — arranged in a timeline of birth years from 1930 to 1998. At night the benches illuminate the sky, reflecting in pools of water beneath each bench.

label: Flight 93 National Memorial
sub: A memorial park opened September 10, 2011 near Shanksville, Pennsylvania, honoring the 40 passengers and crew of United Airlines Flight 93. Features a Wall of Names, a Tower of Voices with 40 wind chimes, and a visitor center. The crash site itself is visible from the memorial plaza.

label: St. Paul's Chapel
sub: Built in 1766 and miraculously undamaged despite being across from the WTC complex, St. Paul's Chapel served as a round-the-clock relief and rest center for Ground Zero rescue workers from September 11, 2001 to May 30, 2002. Over 12,000 volunteers staffed the chapel. It now serves as a memorial site.

label: FDNY Headquarters (MetroTech Center)
sub: FDNY Headquarters coordinated the largest firefighting and rescue operation in New York City history. The FDNY lost 343 members on 9/11 — the largest loss of firefighters from a single incident in history. All five boroughs sent personnel to the World Trade Center that morning.

label: NYPD Headquarters (One Police Plaza)
sub: NYPD coordinated the largest police response in New York City history. The department lost 23 officers on 9/11. NYPD and FDNY communications failures — separate radio systems that couldn't communicate with each other — were identified by the 9/11 Commission as a critical gap that cost first responder lives.

label: Langley AFB (F-16s Scrambled)
sub: F-16 fighters from Langley Air Force Base in Virginia were scrambled at 9:24 AM to intercept suspected hijacked aircraft heading toward Washington, D.C. However, the jets were vectored away from Washington initially due to a phantom aircraft report. They were over the capital only after the Pentagon had already been struck.

label: Otis ANG Base (Cape Cod)
sub: F-15 fighters were scrambled from Otis Air National Guard Base in Massachusetts at 8:52 AM after NORAD was notified about Flight 11. The jets were still 71 miles from New York City when the South Tower was struck at 9:03 AM. NORAD's delayed notification and response was critically examined by the 9/11 Commission.

label: White House (Evacuated)
sub: The White House was evacuated at approximately 9:45 AM on September 11. Vice President Cheney was moved to the Presidential Emergency Operations Center (PEOC) in the basement. The White House, believed to be a potential target, remained a focal point of national command authority throughout the crisis. Flight 93's intended target may have been either here or the Capitol.

label: One World Trade Center
sub: The rebuilt centerpiece of the World Trade Center complex opened November 3, 2014, at the symbolic height of 1,776 feet — the tallest building in the Western Hemisphere. The tower features reinforced concrete walls, blast-resistant lobby design, and redundant stairways, embodying post-9/11 safety lessons.
